title: Where does the ResultSet cursor start?

mcq.question_md: You run a `SELECT` that definitely returns rows, then immediately call `rs.getString("name")` on the `ResultSet` — and get an exception. What did you forget?
options: Calling rs.next() first — the cursor starts before the first row
Calling rs.open() to activate the result set
Nothing — getString only works with column numbers, not names
Sorting the results — an unsorted ResultSet can't be read
mcq.explanation_md: A fresh `ResultSet` cursor sits **above** row one. You must call `rs.next()` to step onto the first row; that's why the standard pattern is `while (rs.next()) { ... }`. Reading before the first `next()` throws, because the cursor isn't on any row yet. The tempting wrong answer is `rs.open()` — no such method exists; the ResultSet is already 'live' when `executeQuery` returns. And `getString` happily accepts either a column name or a column index, so that's not the problem.
